titleEn: Mazzotti era comes to an end within John Deere
titleZh: 迪尔关闭意大利Mazzotti工厂，300M系列自走式喷雾机停产
summaryZh: 约翰迪尔宣布关闭意大利拉文纳的Mazzotti工厂，停止生产Mazzotti品牌及约翰迪尔300M系列自走式喷雾机，原因是销量不佳。该系列在荷兰等欧洲市场因不符合当地漂移减少要求而需求有限。现有用户的零件和服务支持将继续，但Mazzotti品牌未来归属不确定。
featuredReason: 虽然该新闻主要涉及大田作物植保机械，但智慧畜牧行业可从中借鉴设备制造商在技术迭代、区域市场适配及品牌整合方面的经验，对畜牧自动化设备供应商有间接参考价值。
contentFull: Mazzotti era comes to an end within John Deere 04-06 | Autonomous/semi-autosteering systems | NewsLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrintThis John Deere 332M is based on a Mazzotti design. Production of the John Deere 332M and 340M self-propelled sprayers will cease following the closure of the Ravenna factory. Photo: Jacco van ErkelensJohn Deere is closing the Mazzotti factory in Ravenna, Italy, and ending production of both the Mazzotti-branded self-propelled sprayers and the John Deere 300M Series. According to the manufacturer, disappointing sales figures are the main reason behind the decision.For the Dutch market, the immediate impact of the production halt appears limited. According to Willem van der Zwaan, product specialist at Dutch company Kraakman, the John Deere 300M Series never really gained a foothold in the Netherlands.“There are a few machines operating in Belgium, but we never offered the 300M in the Netherlands,” says Van der Zwaan. “Demand was limited, partly because the sprayer did not meet Dutch drift reduction requirements.”Mazzotti had an established presence in the NetherlandsBefore its acquisition by John Deere in 2017, Mazzotti was already active in the Dutch market through agricultural machinery dealer Hogervorst. According to Erik Hogervorst, John Deere focused primarily on the global market when developing the 300M Series, rather than on specific Dutch requirements.“John Deere simply looks at the global market,” says Hogervorst. “The Netherlands, with its different application methods, air-assisted spraying systems and additional drift reduction requirements, is generally regarded internationally as a niche market.”According to Hogervorst, the original Mazzotti machines could be adapted to comply with Dutch regulations. “Special versions could be supplied from Italy that met 97.5% or even 99% drift reduction requirements. As the importer, we also played a role in making those adaptations possible.”Parts and service supportSupport for existing Mazzotti users will remain available for the time being. John Deere has announced that it will continue to provide service and spare parts support. Hogervorst also expects existing customers to remain well supported.“They can continue to rely on us for spare parts and service support. We now have 21 years of experience with Mazzotti, so in that respect we are able to operate fairly independently.”End of the brandWhether Mazzotti will continue as a brand under a new owner remains uncertain. According to Hogervorst, John Deere has clearly chosen to rule out that possibility.Join 17,000+ subscribersSubscribe to our newsletter to stay updated about all the need-to-know content in the agricultural sector, two times a week. translationZh:
约翰迪尔正在关闭位于意大利拉文纳的Mazzotti工厂，并停止生产Mazzotti品牌的自走式喷雾机以及约翰迪尔300M系列。据制造商称，令人失望的销售数据是这一决定背后的主要原因。

对于荷兰市场而言，停产的直接冲击似乎有限。据荷兰公司Kraakman的产品专家Willem van der Zwaan称，约翰迪尔300M系列从未真正在荷兰站稳脚跟。

“有几台机器在比利时运行，但我们从未在荷兰提供过300M系列，”van der Zwaan说。“需求有限，部分原因是该喷雾机不符合荷兰的漂移减少要求。”

**Mazzotti在荷兰已有稳固的市场存在**

在2017年被约翰迪尔收购之前，Mazzotti已通过农机经销商Hogervorst活跃在荷兰市场。据Erik Hogervorst称，约翰迪尔在开发300M系列时主要着眼于全球市场，而非荷兰的具体要求。

“约翰迪尔只是着眼于全球市场，”Hogervorst说。“荷兰因其不同的施药方法、风送喷雾系统以及额外的漂移减少要求，在国际上通常被视为一个利基市场。”

据Hogervorst称，最初的Mazzotti机器可以进行改造以符合荷兰法规。“可以从意大利供应符合97.5%甚至99%漂移减少要求的特殊版本。作为进口商，我们也在实现这些改造方面发挥了作用。”

**零件和服务支持**

对现有Mazzotti用户的支持暂时仍将保持。约翰迪尔已宣布将继续提供维修和备件支持。Hogervorst也预计现有客户将继续得到良好支持。

“他们可以继续依赖我们提供备件和服务支持。我们现在拥有21年的Mazzotti经验，因此在这方面我们能够相当独立地运作。”

**品牌终结**

Mazzotti是否会以新所有者的身份继续作为品牌存在仍不确定。据Hogervorst称，约翰迪尔已明确选择排除这种可能性。
author: Jeroen Poldermans
contentHtml: John Deere is closing the Mazzotti factory in Ravenna, Italy, and ending production of both the Mazzotti-branded self-propelled sprayers and the John Deere 300M Series. According to the manufacturer, disappointing sales figures are the main reason behind the decision.
